source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Centurio autem gubernatori et nauclero magis credebat, quam his quæ a Paulo dicebantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et cum aptus portus non esset ad hiemandum, plurimi statuerunt consilium navigare inde, si quomodo possent, devenientes Phœnicen hiemare, portum Cretæ respicientem ad Africum et ad Corum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Aspirante autem austro, æstimantes propositum se tenere, cum sustulissent de Asson, legebant Cretam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non post multum autem misit se contra ipsam ventus typhonicus, qui vocatur Euroaquilo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque arrepta esset navis, et non posset conari in ventum, data nave flatibus, ferebamur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In insulam autem quamdam decurrentes, quæ vocatur Cauda, potuimus vix obtinere scapham.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qua sublata, adjutoriis utebantur, accingentes navem, timentes ne in Syrtim inciderent, summisso vase sic ferebantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Valida autem nobis tempestate jactatis, sequenti die jactum fecerunt :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Neque autem sole, neque sideribus apparentibus per plures dies, et tempestate non exigua imminente, jam ablata erat spes omnis salutis nostræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et cum multa jejunatio fuisset, tunc stans Paulus in medio eorum, dixit : Oportebat quidem, o viri, audito me, non tollere a Creta, lucrique facere injuriam hanc et jacturam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et nunc suadeo vobis bono animo esse : amissio enim nullius animæ erit ex vobis, præterquam navis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et inde cum audissent fratres, occurrerunt nobis usque ad Appii forum, ac tres Tabernas. Quos cum vidisset Paulus, gratias agens Deo, accepit fiduciam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum constituissent autem illi diem, venerunt ad eum in hospitium plurimi, quibus exponebat testificans regnum Dei, suadensque eis de Jesu ex lege Moysi et prophetis a mane usque ad vesperam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)
